messages.content:
HOW YOUR CLAIM BECOMES A NUMBER (the perturbation contract):
- You never state € impacts. You emit machine-readable ops; the engine computes
  ΔTAM/ΔSAM/ΔYAM by re-running the real funnel. Any € figure you write in prose
  will be stripped.
- Ops: {nodeId, op: "set"|"scale"|"add", value} rewrite one leaf's value;
  {nodeId, op: "exclude"} removes a dimension cell from the venture's reachable
  scope ("unavailable to us" — foreclosure), which is different from shrinking it.
- Ops apply in array order. Ratio nodes must stay inside [0,1]; tamBase must stay > 0.
- Only FUNNEL-ACTIVE nodes may be perturbed (marked in the ledger you receive).
  shape.cagr and shape.cr3 are context — a demand-pace or concentration risk must
  be translated into the funnel nodes it would actually move (tamBase level,
  serviceableFactor reach, obtainableFactor pace, or a share).
- Sibling shares are NOT re-normalized when you move one share (documented v1
  simplification). If you mean a mix-SHIFT, perturb multiple siblings; if you
  mean "this slice is smaller in absolute terms", one op is correct.
- Perturbing an assumption leaf overrides whatever the user set: your claim is
  about the world, not about their slider.

Fix the hypothesis's targetNodes/perturbation so it passes validation WITHOUT changing the claim's meaning. If the claim cannot be expressed against funnel-active nodes, translate it into the funnel nodes it would actually move.

FUNNEL-ACTIVE NODES:
tamBase (EUR_M, value 300)
geo.DE (ratio, value 0.5)
geo.NL (ratio, value 0.28)
geo.PL (ratio, value 0.07)
geo.CH (ratio, value 0.08)
geo.CZ (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.AT (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.other (ratio, value 0.03)
seg.hyperscale (ratio, value 0.44)
seg.colocation (ratio, value 0.18)
seg.enterprise (ratio, value 0.29)
seg.edge (ratio, value 0.05)
seg.telecom (ratio, value 0.04)
cust.operator-large (ratio, value 0.4)
cust.operator-mid (ratio, value 0.25)
cust.oem (ratio, value 0.22)
cust.distributor (ratio, value 0.13)
serviceableFactor (ratio, value 0.55)
obtainableFactor (ratio, value 0.01)

HYPOTHESIS:
{
 "id": "enefg-eed-reporting-spec-creep-dcim-lock",
 "title": "EnEfG §12 + EED Delegated Reg 2024/1364 reporting duties spec-crawl into PDU tenders as certified-accuracy + incumbent-DCIM-integration requirements",
 "narrative": "Germany's Energy Efficiency Act obliges data centers to file energy and efficiency data to the Energieeffizienzregister annually (31 March), and the EU's 2024/1364 rating scheme adds a parallel Union-level reporting layer (annual 15 May filings). Neither instrument names PDUs — which is precisely why the model ignores them. But operators discharge these duties through their monitoring chain, so 2026 PDU specifications in Germany and beyond increasingly require certified metering accuracy classes plus validated integration with the incumbent DCIM suites (EcoStruxure, Vertiv Environet, Sunbird) that generate the filings. A new entrant's PDU without a certified accuracy declaration and pre-validated DCIM connectors fails the spec even when its own telemetry is superior — the reporting laws convert the incumbents' software ecosystems into a de facto certification the entrant lacks. The serviceableFactor's 0.55 was set against a product-law cert stack; it does not price an ecosystem-integration gate that binds hardest in the 50% German cell.",
 "category": "model-structure",
 "targetNodes": [
  "serviceableFactor"
 ],
 "mechanism": [
  {
   "step": "trigger",
   "text": "EnEfG and EU 2024/1364 reporting deadlines recur annually through the venture's window, forcing operators to produce auditable energy data — with German colo already publicizing energy metrics (88% RE share) as a compliance posture",
   "refs": [
    "digest:serviceable-benchmark#c12",
    "digest:serviceable-benchmark#c10",
    "digest:geo-DE#c11"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "propagation",
   "text": "Operators push the compliance burden down into procurement specs: certified metering accuracy and named-DCIM integration become mandatory line items in PDU tenders, and the incumbent suites' validation programs (run by the same top-3 vendors) become gatekeepers for challenger hardware",
   "refs": [
    "digest:refine-competitive-foreclosure.oem-cell-odm-price-foreclosure#c5",
    "ledger:serviceableFactor",
    "digest:serviceable-benchmark#c11"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "model-impact",
   "text": "The serviceable share of TAM for an unvalidated entrant sits below the 0.45 band floor in reporting-bound geographies — and the ledger itself concedes the 0.55 has no bottom-up cross-check to catch this",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:serviceableFactor",
    "digest:serviceable-benchmark#c3",
    "ledger:geo.DE"
   ]
  }
 ],
 "whyMissable": "Both instruments bind OPERATORS, not vendors — a founder scanning for laws that name their product finds nothing and files these under 'customer's problem'. The transmission is contractual spec language, not law, so it never appears in a regulatory scan; it appears in lost tenders, one quarter too late.",
 "expectedObviousness": "medium",
 "falsifier": "A sample of 2026 German/Dutch colo PDU specifications showing no certified-accuracy or named-DCIM-integration requirements, or evidence that major DCIM suites accept open-protocol (SNMP/Redfish) PDU data for EnEfG/EED filings without vendor-specific validation.",
 "likelihood": {
  "value": 0.3,
  "rationale": "The reporting obligations and deadlines are enacted; the spec-creep transmission into PDU tenders is plausible and consistent with documented integrator behavior (branded-incumbent selection on DCIM compatibility) but not yet directly evidenced at tender level.",
  "basis": "judgment"
 },
 "perturbation": [
  {
   "nodeId": "serviceableFactor",
   "op": "set",
   "value": 0.45,
   "note": "Reporting-bound demand requiring validated DCIM integration exits the entrant's reachable pool"
  }
 ],
 "indicators": [
  {
   "signal": "Accuracy-class and DCIM-integration clauses in CE colo PDU specifications",
   "where": "German colo RFQs and framework specs (Frankfurt operators); Bundesstelle für Energieeffizienz / Energieeffizienzregister guidance updates on required data granularity",
   "threshold": "Rack/outlet-level certified metering named in register guidance or ≥2 tenders",
   "updates": "increases"
  },
  {
   "signal": "Openness of incumbent DCIM validation programs to third-party PDUs",
   "where": "Schneider EcoStruxure and Vertiv Environet partner/compatibility lists; Sunbird supported-device database",
   "threshold": "Third-party PDU additions stalling or program fees/queues lengthening",
   "updates": "increases"
  },
  {
   "signal": "EU 2024/1364 second-phase requirements",
   "where": "Commission delegated-act docket for the data-centre rating scheme",
   "threshold": "Phase-2 draft mandating sub-facility (rack-level) energy granularity",
   "updates": "increases"
  }
 ],
 "mitigations": [
  {
   "action": "Audit 10 recent German/Dutch colo PDU specs for accuracy-class and DCIM clauses to size the gated fraction of the serviceable pool",
   "type": "information",
   "voiNodeId": "serviceableFactor",
   "note": null
  },
  {
   "action": "Prioritize certification into the two dominant DCIM compatibility programs and ship native Redfish/SNMP profiles mapped to EnEfG/EED reporting fields as a product feature",
   "type": "operational",
   "voiNodeId": null,
   "note": "Makes compliance reporting a selling point instead of a gate"
  },
  {
   "action": "Co-market with a DCIM-independent monitoring vendor targeting operators who resent incumbent suite lock-in",
   "type": "strategic",
   "voiNodeId": null,
   "note": null
  }
 ],
 "lens": "regulatory-gauntlet",
 "riskId": "risk.regulatory-gauntlet.enefg-eed-reporting-spec-creep-dcim-lock"
}

VALIDATION ERROR:
[
  {
    "code": "invalid_value",
    "values": [
      "error",
      "risk"
    ],
    "path": [
      0,
      "resolution"
    ],
    "message": "Invalid option: expected one of \"error\"|\"risk\""
  }
]

Emit the corrected hypothesis JSON (same shape).
